Sandisk 32GB SD Card Class 10: (Just starting out, this would be a great kit. It even comes

Gear On A Tight Budget ($100-300)
This will require your Smartphone for shooting video. We will need a tripod and a
microphone to complete this set up. For lighting, you could use good daylight or the LED
lighting kit from the previous set-up.

2 different diagrams, one with lights and one without
Video Editing Software
We use various NLE (non-linear editing) software depending on the needs for the video. The
basics of video editing, aka the language, of video editing is consistent across many types of
software. The needs of your project will often dictate the software you’d prefer to use.
www.getwsodo.com

-- 8 of 10 --

Final Cut Pro X - This is one of our main video editing applications for SM content. This
software is only for Apple computers. This software is a single time purchase of about $300 and
is available in the app store.
Adobe Premiere Pro CC 2018 - We also use Adobe Premiere Pro CC, especially when we are
working on a project between multiple editors. This software is available for both Apple and
Windows machines. This software is available on subscription, for pricing plans please visit
Adobe’s website at https://www.adobe.com/.
ScreenFlow - We use this software when we need to record our screens with a voiceover. This
software is a helpful tool for making videos quickly, but is geared towards a more casual video
editor.
There are also other types of video editing software available for free. For example, Apple users
have iMovie and Windows users have Windows Movie Maker.
Whatever editor you choose, become fluent in it. Learn the key commands and get your
workflow down. Storytelling is the primary motivation. The audience doesn’t care which editing
software you used, they just want compelling content.
Video Formulas
SmartMarketer is a hybrid of education and entertainment; or “edutainment”. Part of getting this
information to people is doing it in a fun way that not only captures, but hold their attention.
We will be taking a look at our event videos to analyze what makes these videos tick. Typically
our format includes the following:
Pattern Interrupt>Intro (often with b-roll)>An establishing shot(s)/Montage>Content
(layered with B-Roll)>Montage/Fun Surprise>Outro
Pattern Interrupt: This is also known as a “cold open”. This spot is the first few seconds of your
video where you’ve captured your audience's attention. The goal here is to generate enough
curiosity to keep them watching. These are the most effective when they are unusual, zany, or
larger than life. Here’s a recent example of a pattern interrupt we’ve used to promote our latest
blog video: https://ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/2u07k38vka
Intro: This is the part of the video where you introduce yourself to and briefly cover what you
are going to be covering in the video. This will help your audience orient themselves and
determine if the content is relevant enough to them to keep watching. We also use b-roll in here
to show the action and not just talk about it. We are still fighting to keep people watching at this
point: https://ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/9vfaxhn4nm
www.getwsodo.com

-- 9 of 10 --

Establishing Shot(s)/Montage: After you’ve captured their attention and teased out what is to
come in your video, you can begin to show them. This is the purpose of the establishing shot. It
serves for further orient your viewer while providing a POV into the experience. https://
ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/pgdorobica
Content Layered with B-Roll: This is where the main content of the video is held. In these
examples being shown, the main content is usually an interview with someone. This is where
you really need to deliver the goods if you want to build your audience. Present value to others
and they will (hopefully) come back for more. https://ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/jdlfdda47w
Your b-roll will service you with 2 purposes: a functional, and a narrative. Functionally, your b-roll
will cover up edits you’ve made in your video. Narratively, they should serve as a means to
augment your story. In other words, you need to Show with your B-Roll instead of relying on
Telling your audience what they need to know. If need be, b-roll could even be edited together
and still be able to communicate a complete story. Here’s 3 examples of this:
https://ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/akynvywekj 2018 - March 28-30th - Conversion XL
https://ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/70tgw5hubg 2018_Resonate 2018- ATL, GA.2
https://ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/lqtegvxe8q 2018- February 26-28th - T&C 2018
Notice how these edits are around 30 seconds a piece, but each of them feel like they have a
beginning, middle, and end. Strong B-Roll will make strong videos.
Montage/Fun Surprise: This is a bridge between your content and your outro. This spot is also
a good spot to put any social proof you may have. To put it into story terms, this is the resolution
taking place before the end.
Notice how in this example, the person talking with Ezra says he can’t wait to see him on stage.
That informed the editing of the video and created an motivated opportunity to show Ezra on
stage and his meet-&-greet after his talk at 10x. This led us perfectly to our outro. 	https://
ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/0a77eswa38
Outro: The end of the journey. This is where you recap what you’ve covered in the video and
convey any call to action you’d like your view to complete. If they’ve made it this far, this means
you’ve been able to successfully accomplish creating an engaging video.
Notice how this outro Ezra is able to weave the content of the video and relay a pitch for his
mastermind in an authentic way, as well as inform viewers what more you could expect from
him in the future.
This is authentic selling done right.
https://ezrafirestone.wistia.com/medias/vos6av49ve
www.getwsodo.com

-- 10 of 10 --
